title: paeonia officinalis subsp. humilis
intro: Paeonia officinalis subsp. humilis, also known as the dwarf peony, is a perennial plant species within the Paeonia officinalis complex, characterized by its low-growing habit and fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, as well as its potential to exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Paeonia officinalis subsp. humilis are attributed to its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and anxiety, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions, including menopausal symptoms and insomnia. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate menstrual cramps.
